div.p: Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land greeting. Know that we have conceded to Geoffrey of Farsley, our knight and sworn man, that he shall have and hold his lands that he holds of us in feu at Crail in free Warren. Wherefore we strictly forbid that anyone may fell timber or hunt within the same lands without licence of the same Geoffrey, on pain of our full forfeiture of ten pounds. As witnesses: Robert, abbot of Dunfermline; Robert Menzies and William Oliphant. At Dunfermline, on the 16th day of April, in the 30th year of the reign of the lord King.
